failure_modes.text: Teams can mistake heavy effort for real progress even when rivals' simultaneous adaptation is canceling out the gain.
failure_modes.source_quote: effort does not guarantee progress; intense effort (running) is merely the cost of maintaining a relative status
failure_modes.mitigation: Measure progress against the competitive landscape, not against your own baseline: ask whether the gap to rivals has actually widened, not just whether internal metrics improved.

failure_modes.text: Continuous adaptation pressure can mutate into analysis paralysis or feature creep when the team never stabilizes on the real problem.
failure_modes.source_quote: The pursuit of continuous adaptation risks getting mired in endless questioning, leading to analysis paralysis rather than effective action.
failure_modes.mitigation: Set a stability checkpoint: after each adaptation cycle, decide whether to lock the current approach and exploit it or continue adapting, to prevent perpetual churn.

failure_modes.text: Old successful mental models can survive too long in a changing environment and become the reason adaptation fails.
failure_modes.mitigation: Schedule regular model retirement reviews: audit which legacy assumptions and playbooks have been outrun by environmental change and retire them before they cause adaptation failure.

premortem_questions.text: What would need to be true for this idea to work in a baseline that is still moving under us?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would need to be true for this idea to work?

premortem_questions.text: Is the worst case bad enough if adaptation pressure drives us below acceptable safety thresholds?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.text: What are we actually trying to say here, and what is the single purpose of this adaptation?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What am I actually trying to say here? What is the single purpose of this adaptation?

heuristics.text: Treat anti-complacency as an execution discipline, not just as a motivational slogan.
heuristics.source_quote: focusing on implementation disciplines (execution) to bridge the gap

heuristics.text: Use attack-team and defense-team style simulation to anticipate countermoves instead of assuming the environment stays still.
heuristics.source_quote: breaking a team into an attack and a defense team

heuristics.text: Build continuous feedback loops so adaptation is guided by learning rather than by panic or habit.

curation_notes.summary: The source is strongest on anti-complacency, moving baselines, and adaptation discipline rather than on generic competitive hustle.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor rivalry rhetoric beyond the raw source's clearer feedback, execution, and anti-entrenchment material.
open_questions: The first failure mode compresses the source's Lewis Carroll analogy into a reusable operational warning about relative progress.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on first-principles-thinking, outside-view, and systemic feedback-loops.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is strongest on confirmation-bias, margin-of-safety, and availability-style frame substitution.
structured_tensions: The source contains a clean current-vocabulary conflict with margin-of-safety and source-real pressure against confirmation-bias.
